message:
fix(auth): include error details in OAuth token refresh failure messages

When an OAuth token refresh fails for a provider (both standard and
extension providers), the error message pushed to failed_providers now
includes the actual error via format!("{provider} ({e})") instead of
just the provider name. This makes diagnostic output actionable — users
and logs can see *why* a refresh failed (expired token, network error,
invalid grant, etc.) without needing to reproduce the failure.

Two call sites changed:
- AuthStorage::refresh_oauth_tokens (standard providers, line ~603)
- AuthStorage::refresh_extension_oauth_tokens (extension providers, line ~696)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
